summary_text:
Chief Complaint
Right elbow pain following a lacrosse injury.

History of Present Illness
The patient reports right elbow pain that began approximately one week ago following a collision during a lacrosse game while playing with his children. He describes the pain as sharp and localized to the tip of the elbow, exacerbated by movement. He denies any specific mechanism of injury beyond being checked by his son. He took Advil for a couple of days, but it didn't provide significant relief. He does not recall falling, but his son was rough with him. He reports the pain radiates down the forearm. The patient's son was checking him several times with a sick look.

Findings
Physical examination reveals tenderness to palpation at the olecranon process of the right elbow. Range of motion is limited with flexion and extension, producing pain. A grade 2/6 systolic ejection murmur is present. The x-ray is normal, showing no fracture or bony abnormality. Lung sounds are clear bilaterally. The heart exam is normal.

Assessment
The patient's symptoms and physical exam findings are consistent with a right elbow contusion and associated inflammation.

Plan
I am prescribing 800 milligrams of ibuprofen to be taken twice daily for pain and inflammation. I also recommend icing the area three times a day for 20 minutes. For the next couple of weeks, I recommend you refrain from playing lacrosse with your son. We will obtain an A1C to assess his diabetes control and adjust metformin dosage as needed. Please follow up in four weeks if symptoms persist.
reference_summary:
CHIEF COMPLAINT

Right elbow pain.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS

Cardiovascular: Denies chest pain.
Gastrointestinal: Denies abdominal pain.
Musculoskeletal: Reports right elbow pain.

VITALS

Blood pressure: 150/90 mmHg

PHYSICAL EXAM

Respiratory
- Auscultation of Lungs: Clear bilaterally.

Cardiovascular
Grade 2/6 systolic ejection murmur.

Musculoskeletal
- Examination: Pain to palpation of the olecranon area of the posterior elbow. Mild pain with flexion and extension but otherwise normal range of motion. There is swelling about the elbow, as well as mild ecchymosis.

Hematology/Lymphatic/Immunology
- Examination: Bruising noted over the right elbow.

RESULTS

X-ray of the right elbow was reviewed and demonstrates no evidence of fracture or bony abnormalities.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

1. Right elbow contusion.
- Medical Reasoning: The patient sustained an injury to his elbow when his son struck him with a lacrosse stick. His x-ray and exam findings are consistent with an elbow contusion.
- Patient Education and Counseling: I encouraged the patient to avoid playing lacrosse for the next couple of weeks, just until his elbow heals. After that, he should be fine to continue playing normally.
- Medical Treatment: I'm going to prescribe him ibuprofen 800 mg twice daily to help with some of the pain and swelling. He should also ice the area in 20-minute intervals 3 times per day to further reduce his symptoms.

2. Hypertension.
- Medical Reasoning: This appears to be well-controlled with dietary modifications and lisinopril.
- Patient Education and Counseling: I encouraged him to keep up with his dietary modifications.
- Medical Treatment: We will keep him on lisinopril 20 mg daily.

3. Diabetes.
- Medical Reasoning: This appears to be well controlled at this time.
- Medical Treatment: We will keep him on metformin 500 mg and order a hemoglobin A1c to evaluate any need for medication adjustments.

Patient Agreements: The patient understands and agrees with the recommended medical treatment plan.

section_miscategorizations.text: A grade 2/6 systolic ejection murmur is present.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This cardiac exam finding conflicts with the later statement that the heart exam is normal, creating a clinically misleading inconsistency within the Findings section. One of these statements is effectively miscategorized or erroneous, but the note structure does not clarify which is correct.

This statement directly contradicts the documented systolic ejection murmur in the same Findings section. The mutually exclusive findings create a misleading representation of the physical exam; one of these entries is effectively misdocumented for the section's purpose.

section_miscategorizations.text: We will obtain an A1C to assess his diabetes control and adjust metformin dosage as needed.
section_miscategorizations.reason: While orders and medication adjustments belong in the Plan, there is no prior mention of diabetes or metformin in HPI, Findings, or Assessment. The lack of corresponding problem assessment makes this plan item feel organizationally misplaced relative to the rest of the note's content.
